description_long: This is a lyre-shaped harp with a dark, polished wooden frame featuring intricate carved patterns along its outer surface. The frame forms a wide, symmetrical U-shape, with two upward-curving arms connected by a horizontal crossbar at the top. Six evenly spaced strings run vertically from the crossbar to the base, each taut and parallel, likely made of metal or gut. The wood exhibits a rich, reddish-brown hue with visible grain and a glossy finish, suggesting a high-quality varnish. The instrument is compact and lightweight, suitable for handheld use, and its decorative carvings add an elegant, classical aesthetic. No soundboard is visible, and the back is flat, emphasizing the open, airy design typical of ancient lyres.
description: A compact, lyre-shaped harp with a dark, carved wooden frame and six vertical strings, featuring ornate detailing and a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full lyre shape, six strings, and detailed carved patterns on the wooden frame.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the harp and the absence of a visible soundboard.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the symmetrical frame and string arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and carved frame edges.
attribute_frame_design: The frame is lyre-shaped, forming a wide U with upward-curving arms and a horizontal crossbar, adorned with carved decorative motifs.
attribute_column_curvature: The column is gently curved, following a smooth, symmetrical U-shape with no sharp bends or twists.
